controls.control_id: Threat model the agent pipeline for adversarial goal manipulation vectors including direct injection, indirect injection via tool outputs, and context poisoning
controls.title: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
controls.description: Ensures goal integrity is a design-phase requirement for all agentic systems

controls.control_id: Review agent behaviour for goal deviation — verify that the agent maintains intended objectives under adversarial input conditions
controls.title: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Catches goal manipulation vulnerabilities before production deployment

controls.control_id: Conduct adversarial red-team testing against goal hijacking vectors including injection through every data source, tool output, and context channel
controls.title: PW.8.2-PS – Test for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Validates goal integrity controls under realistic attack conditions

controls.control_id: Establish procedures to identify goal hijacking incidents in production including goal deviation monitoring, triage, and confirmation workflows
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables rapid detection and response to goal manipulation in live systems

controls.control_id: Define explicit security requirements specifying the maximum permitted tool access, API scope, data source access, and resource boundaries for each agent deployment
controls.title: PW.1.1-PS – Define security requirements
controls.description: Establishes access control as a mandatory deployment requirement

controls.control_id: Threat model all agent access paths to tools, data stores, and APIs; design least-privilege tool manifests and enforce tenant isolation by design
controls.title: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
controls.description: Ensures access boundaries are designed before implementation

controls.control_id: Protect agent configuration files, tool manifests, permission policies, and orchestration definitions from unauthorised modification
controls.title: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
controls.description: Prevents tampering with access control configuration

controls.control_id: Review agent access control enforcement — verify that tool permission manifests, RBAC policies, and tenant isolation boundaries are correctly implemented and cannot be bypassed
controls.title: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Validates access controls before production deployment

controls.control_id: Define explicit privilege boundaries for each agent identity — maximum permitted privilege level, credential scope, and escalation constraints
controls.title: PW.1.1-PS – Define security requirements
controls.description: Establishes privilege boundaries as mandatory requirements

controls.control_id: Implement secure credential handling — agents must not inherit user credentials, store tokens in context, or pass credentials between agents without explicit authorisation
controls.title: PW.5.1-PS – Secure coding practices
controls.description: Prevents credential leakage through agent code paths

controls.control_id: Protect credential stores, identity configurations, and privilege mapping files from unauthorised access and modification
controls.title: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
controls.description: Prevents tampering with privilege boundaries

controls.control_id: Establish monitoring and triage procedures for privilege escalation incidents — detect agents operating beyond their assigned privilege level
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables rapid detection of privilege escalation in production

controls.control_id: Vet all third-party agent components — tools, plugins, MCP servers, model weights, orchestration libraries — for provenance, integrity, and security posture before use
controls.title: PW.4.1-PS – Reuse existing well-secured software
controls.description: Prevents introduction of compromised components into agent pipelines

controls.control_id: Verify integrity of all agent artefacts and third-party components using cryptographic signatures and checksums before deployment
controls.title: PS.2.1-PS – Verify software integrity
controls.description: Detects tampering in agent supply chain artefacts

controls.control_id: Maintain a secure, versioned registry of all agent components with provenance records; enable auditability and rollback
controls.title: PS.3.1-PS – Archive and protect software releases
controls.description: Ensures traceability and recovery capability for supply chain incidents

controls.control_id: Monitor for newly disclosed vulnerabilities in third-party agent components; establish a triage process for AI-specific supply chain disclosures
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables rapid response to supply chain compromises

controls.control_id: Threat model all code execution paths in agent workflows; design sandboxing, resource limits, and execution constraints as explicit security requirements
controls.title: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
controls.description: Ensures code execution boundaries are designed before implementation

controls.control_id: Implement secure coding for agent code execution — sandbox isolation, input validation for code generation, output filtering, and prevention of self-modification
controls.title: PW.5.1-PS – Secure coding practices
controls.description: Prevents code execution vulnerabilities in agent implementation

controls.control_id: Conduct adversarial testing targeting code execution — sandbox escapes, resource limit bypasses, self-modification, and host system access through generated code
controls.title: PW.8.2-PS – Test for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Validates execution boundary controls under attack conditions

controls.control_id: Protect agent execution environments, sandbox configurations, and runtime constraints from unauthorised modification
controls.title: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
controls.description: Prevents weakening of execution boundaries through configuration tampering

controls.control_id: Protect agent memory stores, context databases, and shared state repositories from unauthorised read, write, and modification; enforce access controls per agent identity
controls.title: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
controls.description: Prevents direct tampering with agent memory and context

controls.control_id: Maintain versioned, integrity-verified snapshots of agent memory and context stores; enable rollback to pre-poisoning states
controls.title: PS.3.1-PS – Archive and protect software releases
controls.description: Ensures recovery capability for memory poisoning incidents

controls.control_id: Review agent behaviour for memory-influenced anomalies — verify that persistent memory and shared context do not introduce unintended behaviour changes across sessions
controls.title: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Catches memory poisoning effects before they propagate

controls.control_id: When memory poisoning is detected, conduct forensic analysis to identify the poisoned records, their ingestion source, propagation path, and blast radius across agents
controls.title: RV.3.1-PS – Analyse root causes
controls.description: Enables thorough incident response for memory poisoning events

controls.control_id: Define explicit security requirements constraining permitted tool invocation sequences and cross-tool data flows for each agent deployment
controls.title: PW.1.1-PS – Define security requirements
controls.description: Establishes chaining constraints as mandatory requirements

controls.control_id: Threat model tool interaction graphs — identify composite action sequences that could achieve unauthorised outcomes; design controls for chain-level authorisation
controls.title: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
controls.description: Ensures chaining risks are addressed at design time

controls.control_id: Review agent behaviour for chain-based scope violations — verify that multi-step tool sequences cannot achieve outcomes exceeding individual tool permissions
controls.title: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Catches chaining vulnerabilities before production

controls.control_id: Establish monitoring for anomalous tool invocation sequences; define triage procedures for suspected lateral chaining incidents
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables detection of chaining attacks in production

controls.control_id: Design circuit breakers, step limits, cost budgets, and human approval gates as explicit security requirements for all agentic automation workflows
controls.title: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
controls.description: Ensures cascade prevention is a design-phase requirement

controls.control_id: Conduct adversarial testing of cascade failure paths — test error propagation, hallucination amplification, and runaway automation scenarios
controls.title: PW.8.2-PS – Test for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Validates cascade prevention controls under attack conditions

controls.control_id: Define remediation procedures for cascade failure incidents including automatic circuit breaker activation, workflow suspension, cost cap enforcement, and rollback
controls.title: RV.2.1-PS – Assess, prioritise, and remediate vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables rapid response to cascading automation failures

controls.control_id: Define explicit requirements for maximum automation depth, step limits, cost budgets, and mandatory human checkpoints for each agent workflow
controls.title: PW.1.1-PS – Define security requirements
controls.description: Establishes automation boundaries as mandatory requirements

controls.control_id: Review agent behaviour for emergent capabilities — verify that self-modification, dynamic tool discovery, and autonomous agent spawning do not create unintended security exposures
controls.title: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Catches emergent pattern risks before production

controls.control_id: Conduct adversarial testing targeting emerging agentic patterns — self-evolution, prompt self-modification, autonomous tool acquisition, and dynamic agent creation
controls.title: PW.8.2-PS – Test for security vulnerabilities
controls.description: Validates controls against novel attack surfaces

controls.control_id: Establish monitoring for emergent agent behaviours — detect agents acquiring new capabilities, modifying their own definitions, or spawning sub-agents outside approved patterns
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables detection of emerging risks in production

controls.control_id: When incidents involve novel agentic patterns, conduct root cause analysis focused on understanding the emergent capability and its security implications
controls.title: RV.3.1-PS – Analyse root causes
controls.description: Builds organisational knowledge of emerging agentic risks

controls.control_id: Vet all external agent dependencies — LLM APIs, tool endpoints, MCP servers, orchestration platforms — for reliability, security posture, and failure mode characteristics before adoption
controls.title: PW.4.1-PS – Reuse existing well-secured software
controls.description: Prevents adoption of unreliable dependencies

controls.control_id: Verify that external dependency responses are consistent with expected behaviour — detect API version changes, model swaps, or degraded output quality that could affect agent correctness
controls.title: PS.2.1-PS – Verify software integrity
controls.description: Detects dependency degradation and tampering

controls.control_id: Monitor all agent dependencies for availability, behavioural consistency, and security posture changes; establish triage procedures for dependency degradation events
controls.title: RV.1.1-PS – Identify and confirm vulnerabilities
controls.description: Enables rapid detection of dependency failures

controls.control_id: Define remediation procedures for dependency failures — graceful degradation, fallback providers, workflow suspension, and stakeholder notification
controls.title: RV.2.1-PS – Assess, prioritise, and remediate vulnerabilities
controls.description: Ensures operational continuity during dependency outages

controls.control_id: PS.1.1-PS
controls.title: Protect all code from unauthorised access — data access audit controls
controls.description: Implement comprehensive audit logging for all access to training data, model weights, embedding stores, and pipeline configuration; enforce tamper-evident log storage; Implement controls to detect and prevent unauthorised AI tool usage that processes organisational data outside governed pipelines; Protect training data, model weights, adapters, and pipeline artefacts from unauthorised modification; enforce write access controls and integrity monitoring

controls.control_id: RV.1.1-PS
controls.title: Identify and confirm vulnerabilities — audit-driven detection
controls.description: Establish procedures to detect data access anomalies using audit logs; define triage workflows for suspicious access patterns across AI data stores; Establish procedures to identify data exposure from shadow AI tool usage; define triage and remediation workflows for unsanctioned data processing; Establish procedures to detect data misuse and manipulation in production including monitoring for purpose-scope violations and data manipulation patterns

controls.control_id: PW.1.1-PS
controls.title: Define security requirements — data inventory and classification
controls.description: Define security requirements mandating a complete data inventory covering all datasets used in training, fine-tuning, RAG, and evaluation; classify by sensitivity; Define security requirements governing how datasets may be combined for AI training and inference; require aggregation impact assessments before merging datasets; Define security requirements governing data retention periods, deletion procedures, and right-to-erasure compliance for all data in AI pipelines

controls.control_id: PW.2.1-PS
controls.title: Design software — data flow transparency
controls.description: Design AI pipelines with documented data flow diagrams showing data origins, transformations, storage locations, and access points; maintain as living artefacts; Design AI data pipelines with built-in lineage tracking; require lineage metadata capture at every transformation step as an explicit design requirement; Design AI data pipelines with aggregation controls that prevent combination of datasets whose joint sensitivity exceeds authorised classification levels

controls.control_id: PW.4.1-PS
controls.title: Reuse existing well-secured software — AI tool vetting
controls.description: Vet all AI tools, services, and plugins before organisational adoption; verify data handling, security posture, and compliance capabilities; Vet all third-party datasets, pre-trained models, and pipeline components for provenance, integrity, and potential poisoning before use; Vet all datasets for quality, completeness, representativeness, and fitness for purpose before use in any training or evaluation pipeline

controls.control_id: PS.3.1-PS
controls.title: Archive and protect software releases — versioned artefact management
controls.description: Maintain versioned, integrity-verified snapshots of all training data, model checkpoints, and pipeline artefacts; enable rollback to known-good state; Maintain versioned dataset snapshots with provenance metadata recording origin, collection method, processing steps, and quality metrics; Maintain end-to-end data lineage records linking every model version to its training data, transformations, and intermediate artefacts

controls.control_id: RV.3.1-PS
controls.title: Analyse root causes — poisoning forensics
controls.description: When poisoning is detected, conduct forensic analysis to identify corrupted records, trace to source, and determine blast radius across dependent models; When model failures trace to synthetic training data, conduct root cause analysis of the generation process, source data, and privacy mechanism; When discriminatory behaviour is identified, conduct root cause analysis tracing bias to specific training data sources, labelling processes, or preprocessing steps

controls.control_id: PS.2.1-PS
controls.title: Verify software integrity — data provenance verification
controls.description: Verify provenance and integrity of all datasets used in training, fine-tuning, and evaluation; maintain cryptographic attestation of data origin and chain of custody; Verify integrity of all third-party data deliveries using checksums, signatures, or schema validation; detect tampering or corruption before pipeline ingestion; Verify that the model artifact serving output has not been tampered with; maintain signed model checksums and verify before deployment

controls.control_id: PW.5.1-PS
controls.title: Secure coding — data handling in AI pipelines
controls.description: Enforce secure coding practices for all data handling in AI pipelines; implement output filtering, PII detection, and data masking before model responses reach consumers; Implement secure coding practices for all code paths that consume LLM output; treat model responses as untrusted input to downstream systems; Enforce secure coding standards prohibiting embedding of credentials, API keys, or sensitive configuration data in system prompts or model context

controls.control_id: PW.7.2-PS
controls.title: Review for security vulnerabilities — data leakage review
controls.description: Include data leakage and memorisation scenarios in pre-release security reviews; verify that outputs cannot reveal training data or sensitive context; Review synthetic data for bias inheritance, privacy leakage, and statistical fidelity before use in training pipelines; verify privacy guarantees are meaningful; Include data misuse and manipulation scenarios in pre-release reviews; verify that purpose limitation controls are enforced and cannot be bypassed

controls.control_id: PW.8.2-PS
controls.title: Test for security vulnerabilities — synthetic data adversarial testing
controls.description: Conduct adversarial testing of synthetic data for membership inference, attribute inference, and reconstruction attacks to validate privacy claims; Conduct adversarial testing for discriminatory outputs; test model behaviour across demographic groups, intersectional categories, and edge cases; Conduct adversarial testing (red-teaming) against prompt injection vectors before each production release; cover direct, indirect, and multimodal injection paths

controls.control_id: RV.2.1-PS
controls.title: Assess, prioritise, and remediate — deletion request remediation
controls.description: Define procedures to assess and remediate data deletion requests including impact analysis on trained models and retraining requirements; Define remediation procedures for availability incidents including model rollback, rate limit tightening, and cost circuit breakers; Define and test remediation procedures for availability incidents — rate limit tightening, model rollback, cost circuit breaker activation
